text: A harness is the engineered control shell around an LLM: prompt assembly, memory retrieval, tool permissions, loop control, guardrails, tracing, evals, and release gates.
Working memory is temporary context RAM. Durable memory must be explicitly written and retrieved.
LLM Ops is the outer improvement loop: trace runs, evaluate quality, observe health, diagnose root causes, and gate releases.
Loop engineering controls repeated tool calls and defines when an agent should stop, ask for input, or fail closed.
